title: Find your starting point
intro: A short German check that gets harder as it goes, so we can start you in the right place. It takes about two minutes and you can retake it any time.

questions.prompt: 'Ich ___ Anna.' (I am Anna)
questions.explanation: With ich the verb sein is bin.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is written with correct German capitalisation?
questions.explanation: German capitalises every noun, and Hund is a noun.

questions.prompt: Which word means the number 7?
questions.explanation: Sieben is 7, while sechs is 6, acht is 8 and neun is 9.

questions.prompt: Which sentence has correct German word order?
questions.explanation: The conjugated verb takes second position, so the subject moves behind it.

questions.prompt: '___ Buch liegt auf dem Tisch.' (the book)
questions.explanation: Buch is neuter, so the definite article in the nominative is das.

questions.prompt: 'Du ___ sehr schnell.' (sprechen)
questions.explanation: Sprechen changes its stem vowel in the du form: du sprichst.

questions.prompt: What is the plural of das Haus?
questions.explanation: Haus takes an umlaut plus the plural ending er: die Häuser.

questions.prompt: 'Ich sehe ___ Mann.' (I see the man)
questions.explanation: Sehen takes the accusative, and masculine der becomes den there.

questions.prompt: 'Ich habe ___ Zeit.' (I have no time)
questions.explanation: A noun without an article is negated with kein, and Zeit is feminine, so keine.

questions.prompt: 'Das ist ___ Schwester.' (my sister)
questions.explanation: Schwester is feminine and stands in the nominative, so the possessive is meine.

questions.prompt: 'Er ___ gut Klavier spielen.' (he can play the piano well)
questions.explanation: Modal verbs lose the ending in the er form and change the vowel: er kann.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the separable verb aufstehen correctly?
options: Ich stehe um sieben Uhr auf.
Ich auf stehe um sieben Uhr.
Ich stehe auf um sieben Uhr.
questions.answer: Ich stehe um sieben Uhr auf.
questions.explanation: The prefix splits off and goes to the very end of the main clause.

questions.prompt: 'Kannst du ___ helfen?' (help me)
questions.explanation: Helfen takes the dative, and the dative of ich is mir.

questions.prompt: 'Wir treffen uns ___ Montag.' (on Monday)
questions.explanation: Days of the week take an, which contracts with dem to am.

questions.prompt: How do Germans normally say the time 7:30?
questions.explanation: German counts towards the coming hour, so 7:30 is half of the eighth hour.

questions.prompt: 'Ich hänge das Bild an ___ Wand.' (movement to a place)
questions.explanation: An is a two way preposition, and movement answering wohin takes the accusative.

questions.prompt: 'Wir freuen ___ auf den Urlaub.' (we are looking forward to the holiday)
questions.explanation: The reflexive pronoun for wir is uns.

questions.prompt: 'Ich war heute ___ Arzt.' (at the doctor's)
questions.explanation: Being at a person's place takes bei, which contracts with dem to beim.

questions.prompt: Which sentence has the correct word order after dass?
options: Ich weiß, dass er morgen kommt.
Ich weiß, dass er kommt morgen.
Ich weiß, dass kommt er morgen.
Ich weiß, dass morgen er kommt.
questions.answer: Ich weiß, dass er morgen kommt.
questions.explanation: In a subordinate clause the conjugated verb stands at the end.

questions.prompt: 'Ich frage mich, ___ sie schon da ist.' (whether)
questions.explanation: An indirect yes or no question is introduced by ob.

questions.prompt: 'Es regnet, ___ bleiben wir zu Hause.' (therefore)
questions.explanation: Deshalb states the consequence and keeps the verb in second position.

questions.prompt: 'Gestern ___ ich nach Hamburg gefahren.'
questions.explanation: Verbs of movement such as fahren build the Perfekt with sein.

questions.prompt: What is the past participle of sprechen?
questions.explanation: Sprechen is a strong verb, and its participle is gesprochen.

questions.prompt: 'Als Kind ___ ich oft krank.' (spoken German prefers the simple past here)
questions.explanation: Sein is normally used in the Präteritum even in speech: ich war.

questions.prompt: 'Berlin ist ___ als Bonn.' (bigger)
questions.explanation: The comparative of groß adds an umlaut and the ending er.

questions.prompt: 'Im August ist es hier ___.' (hottest)
questions.explanation: A superlative after the verb sein takes the form am plus the ending sten.

questions.prompt: 'Ich kaufe einen ___ Mantel.' (warm)
questions.explanation: After einen a masculine accusative adjective takes the ending en.

questions.prompt: '___ Sie mir bitte helfen?' (polite request to a stranger)
questions.explanation: The polite Konjunktiv II form used with Sie is könnten.

questions.prompt: '___ ich mehr Zeit hätte, würde ich mehr lesen.' (if)
questions.explanation: A conditional clause is introduced by wenn, while wann only asks about time.

questions.prompt: In a clothes shop you want to try a jacket on. What do you ask?
questions.explanation: Anprobieren means to try on, while ausziehen means to take off.

questions.prompt: 'Das ist der Mann, ___ ich gestern getroffen habe.'
questions.explanation: The relative pronoun is the object of treffen, so the masculine accusative den is used.

questions.prompt: 'Das ist das Auto ___ Bruders.' (my brother's car)
questions.explanation: Possession is expressed with the genitive, and masculine takes meines.

questions.prompt: 'Ich warte ___ den Bus.' (I am waiting for the bus)
questions.explanation: Warten has the fixed preposition auf with the accusative.

questions.prompt: 'Ich habe den ___ gefragt.' (der Student)
questions.explanation: Student belongs to the n declension and adds en in every case except the nominative singular.

questions.prompt: '___ interessierst du dich?' (what are you interested in)
questions.explanation: Sich interessieren takes für, so the question word compound is wofür.

questions.prompt: 'Die Frau, ___ ich geholfen habe, ist Ärztin.'
questions.explanation: Helfen takes the dative, and the feminine dative relative pronoun is der.

questions.prompt: 'Das Haus ___ 1920 gebaut.' (was built)
questions.explanation: The passive in the past is formed with wurde plus the past participle.

questions.prompt: 'Als wir ankamen, ___ der Film schon begonnen.'
questions.explanation: An event before another past event takes the Plusquamperfekt with hatte.

questions.prompt: 'Es regnete stark. ___ sind wir spazieren gegangen.'
questions.explanation: Trotzdem starts a main clause, while obwohl would need a subordinate clause.

questions.prompt: 'Wenn ich früher losgefahren wäre, ___ ich den Zug erreicht.'
questions.explanation: Erreichen takes haben, so the past Konjunktiv II is hätte plus the participle.

questions.prompt: 'Ich ___ mein Auto reparieren.' (I am having my car repaired)
questions.explanation: Lassen plus an infinitive expresses having something done by someone else.

questions.prompt: 'Ich freue mich schon ___, dich zu sehen.'
questions.explanation: Sich freuen auf points forward to the clause, so the compound is darauf.
